patch:
commit 73a351e7d42c1da46a915c7edb7e6697a9dea640
Author: Yash Solanki <67216443+yashnator@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 8 22:28:16 2025 +0530

    [llvm][GVN] Propagate `trunc nuw to i1` equalities (#143273)
    
    This patch adds to GVN's `propagateEquality()` to reason about equality
    constraints through `trunc nuw iN to i1`.
    
    Given:
      %tr = trunc nuw iN %v to i1
    
    We can deduce that if `%tr == true`, then `%v == 1`, and if `%tr ==
    false`, then `%v == 0`. This is valid because `nuw` guarantees that
    truncation didn't lose unsigned bits, so `%v` must have been either 0 or
    1.
    
    The patch adds logic to propagate this information via the GVN worklist.
    This enables further simplification opportunities downstream, such as
    folding redundant stores or conditionals that depend on `%v`.
    
    Includes a test case in `GVN/trunc-nuw-equality.ll`.
    
    Resolves #142744

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/GVN.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/GVN.cpp
index 77ca14f88a83..a0eed31fde79 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/GVN.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/GVN.cpp
@@ -2579,6 +2579,14 @@ bool GVNPass::propagateEquality(Value *LHS, Value *RHS,
 
       continue;
     }
+
+    // Propagate equalities that results from truncation with no unsigned wrap
+    // like (trunc nuw i64 %v to i1) == "true" or (trunc nuw i64 %v to i1) ==
+    // "false"
+    if (match(LHS, m_NUWTrunc(m_Value(A)))) {
+      Worklist.emplace_back(A, ConstantInt::get(A->getType(), IsKnownTrue));
+      continue;
+    }
   }
 
   return Changed;

tests.test_body:
define void @test(ptr %p, i64 %v) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define void @test(
; CHECK-SAME: ptr [[P:%.*]], i64 [[V:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:  [[ENTRY:.*:]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TR:%.*]] = trunc nuw i64 [[V]] to i1
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[TR]], label %[[RET:.*]], label %[[STORE:.*]]
; CHECK:       [[STORE]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    store i64 0, ptr [[P]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
; CHECK:       [[RET]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    store i64 1, ptr [[P]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
entry:
  %tr = trunc nuw i64 %v to i1
  br i1 %tr, label %ret, label %store

store:                                            ; preds = %entry
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void

ret:                                              ; preds = %entry
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void
}
tests.source_program:
define void @test(ptr %p, i64 %v) {
entry:
  %tr = trunc nuw i64 %v to i1
  br i1 %tr, label %ret, label %store

store:                                            ; preds = %entry
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void

ret:                                              ; preds = %entry
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void
}
tests.current_optimized_program:
define void @test(ptr %p, i64 %v) {
entry:
  %tr = trunc nuw i64 %v to i1
  br i1 %tr, label %ret, label %store

store:                                            ; preds = %entry
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void

ret:                                              ; preds = %entry
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define void @test(ptr %p, i64 %v) {
entry:
  %tr = trunc nuw i64 %v to i1
  br i1 %tr, label %ret, label %store

store:                                            ; preds = %entry
  store i64 0, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void

ret:                                              ; preds = %entry
  store i64 1, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void
}

issue.title: Missed optimization of constant folding when using 'trunc nuw i64 %v to i1'
issue.body:
The following reduced IR is derived from https://github.com/rust-lang/regex/blob/1a069b9232c607b34c4937122361aa075ef573fa/regex-automata/src/util/pool.rs#L542.

Godbolt: https://godbolt.org/z/KebcfWbE8 (contains the source code before manual reduce)
alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/XPqJVm

Missed optimization: `store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 8` --> `store i64 0, ptr %p, align 8`

While Alive2 confirms that this transformation is correct, I’m having some difficulty fully understanding the semantic justification—particularly with respect to how trunc nuw i64 %v to i1 constrains %v. I would greatly appreciate any clarification on why this optimization is considered valid.

```llvm
define i64 @src(ptr %p, i64 %v) {
  %trunc = trunc nuw i64 %v to i1
  br i1 %trunc, label %common.ret, label %3

common.ret:                                       ; preds = %1, %3
  ret i64 0

3:                                                ; preds = %1
  store i64 %v, ptr %p, align 8
  br label %common.ret
}
```

expected:
```llvm
define i64 @tgt(ptr %p, i64 %v) {
  %trunc = trunc nuw i64 %v to i1
  br i1 %trunc, label %common.ret, label %3

common.ret:                                       ; preds = %1, %3
  ret i64 0

3:                                                ; preds = %1
  store i64 0, ptr %p, align 8
  br label %common.ret
}
```
issue.author: GINN-Imp

comments.author: yashnator
comments.body:
Hi @GINN-Imp, I think the constraint emerges from truncating `%v` to `i1`.  For `%v` > 1, we get a poison value which is then used in the branch instruction that triggers UB. So we are left with `%v` = 0 or 1, which leads to storing 0 to `%p` in 3.  

Maybe we can optimise this for truncations with `nuw`?

I'm not entirely sure if this reasoning is complete, so I wanted to check whether this direction makes sense before proceeding. Any pointers or confirmation would be really helpful.

comments.author: GINN-Imp
comments.body:

> 
> Maybe we can optimise this for truncations with `nuw`?
> 
> I'm not entirely sure if this reasoning is complete, so I wanted to check whether this direction makes sense before proceeding. Any pointers or confirmation would be really helpful.

Thanks for the explanation! That does sound plausible, though unfortunately I’m not entirely sure either.
